summary: Display last game of the WS during the offseason
description: GIVEN configs have been updated to load the date of the last World Series game

steps.action: WHEN the user navigates to the Games page
steps.result:
THEN the last World Series game is displayed
AND the displayed date is that of the last World Series game

summary: Where to watch - Monthly/yearly subscriber live game FGotD in market - Hero/Featured
description:
GIVEN the user is a monthly/yearly subscriber
AND there is a FGotD scheduled/in-progress in the viewing market of the user
AND the game level blackedOutVideo equals true in MASTepg
AND the game level entitledVideo equals true in MASTepg

steps.action: WHEN the user views the FGotD game in the hero/featured spot
steps.result:
THEN a blackout badge is displayed in the upper left corner of the hero/featured spot
AND the "GEICO FREE" badge does not display
AND "Stream on: <team_broadcast_partner_of_the_users_in-market_location>" is displayed above "Broadcast Networks"

summary: SNLA+ VOD RW Cancel
description:
GIVEN a logged in user is entitled for SNLA+ RSN
AND the user is on the SNLA+ page
AND the user selects a VOD to watch

steps.action:
WHEN the user initiates Rewind
AND presses the "back" button on the controller after a few moments
steps.result:
THEN Rewind stops
AND playback resumes at the point where Rewind was initiated
AND the elapsed time in the scrubber reflects the playhead position

summary: MASN2 VOD RW to the beginning
description:
GIVEN a logged in user is entitled for MASN2 RSN
AND the user is on the MASN2 page
AND the user selects a VOD to watch

steps.action: WHEN the user Rewinds the stream to the beginning
steps.result:
THEN Rewind stops once it reaches the start of the stream
AND playback resumes at the beginning of the stream
AND the elapsed time in the scrubber reflects the playhead position

summary: SNLA+ 24/7 feed Pause/Play
description:
GIVEN a logged in user is entitled for SNLA+ RSN
AND the user is playing back the SNLA+ 24/7 stream

steps.action:
WHEN the user presses the "select" button on the controller
AND the user presses the "select" button again after a a minute
steps.result:
THEN video playback pauses after the first button press
AND video playback resumes after the second button press
AND the elapsed time in the scrubber reflects the playhead position
AND the live indicator is grey, indicating not at the live point
AND the "Jump to Live" button displays right justified above the scrubber bar

summary: RSN Carousel navigation to the left
description:
GIVEN a user is on a RSN page
AND focus is assigned to a carousel

steps.action: WHEN the user presses the left arrow on the controller
steps.result:
THEN focus remains on the furthest left full tile
AND the tile that was previously to the left of the focused tile is now in the focused position of the carousel
AND focus remains on the tile when the user is focused on the first tile in the carousel
AND the user can no longer continue navigating left through the carousel once the beginning of the carousel has been reached

summary: Where to watch - Monthly/yearly subscriber live game in market - Hero/Featured
description:
GIVEN the user is a monthly/yearly subscriber
AND there is a game scheduled or in-progress in the viewing market of the user
AND/OR the game level blackedOutVideo equals true in MASTepg
AND the game is not a partner game (ex: AppleTV+)

steps.action: WHEN the user views the game in the featured/hero spot on the Home page
steps.result:
THEN a blackout badge is displayed in the top left of the featured/hero spot
AND "Stream on: <team_broadcast_partner_of_the_users_in-market_location>" is displayed above "Broadcast Networks"
AND after selecting the featured image, a "Where To Watch" modal displays with the in market users viewing option

summary: RSN VOD Playback
description: GIVEN a logged in user is entitled for RSN VOD content

steps.action: WHEN the user selects RSN VOD content for playback from an RSN page
steps.result: THEN the RSN VOD content plays back from the beginning of the video

summary: SNLA+ VOD FF Stop
description:
GIVEN a logged in user is entitled for SNLA+ RSN
AND the user is on the SNLA+ page
AND the user selects a VOD to watch

steps.action:
WHEN the user initiates Fast Forward
AND presses the "select" button on the controller after a few moments
steps.result:
THEN Fast Forward stops
AND playback resumes at the point where Fast Forward stopped
AND the elapsed time in the scrubber reflects the playhead position

summary: IMA SDK is not using Publica Service - Live
description:
GIVEN a Live game stream is playing
AND a domestic user is logged in

steps.action:
WHEN the user selects a Live in-progress game for playback
AND an ad break occurs
AND entering "stream" into the Network search of Dev Tools
AND entering "publica" into the Network search of Dev Tools
steps.result:
THEN ads playback during the break
AND the "streams" call has {{pubads.g.doubleclick.net*}} as the request URL
AND the "publica" call is not made

summary: MASN2 24/7 feed FF to Live Point
description:
GIVEN a logged in user is entitled for MASN2 RSN
AND the user selects the MASN2 24/7 stream to watch
AND the playhead is behind the live point

steps.action: WHEN the user Fast Forwards the stream to the live point
steps.result:
THEN Fast Forward stops when it reaches the live point
AND playback resumes at the live point
AND the Live indicator is red
AND the "Jump to Live" button does not display
AND the elapsed time in the scrubber reflects the playhead position

summary: Hero/Featured Space UI - Game (Pre-Game Hide Spoilers ON)
description:
GIVE a user has launched the MLB.tv app

AND Hide Spoilers is set to ON

AND a game in a Pre-Game state is present in the Hero/Featured Space



NOTE: Figma designs for Card States: [https://www.figma.com/design/rGAWoDJL0fx7RTxv0er9U5/MLB.TV-Connected-Device-Developer-Spec?node-id=179-99561&t=2GMcHnOGaIH6wtRZ-0|https://www.figma.com/design/rGAWoDJL0fx7RTxv0er9U5/MLB.TV-Connected-Device-Developer-Spec?node-id=179-99561&t=2GMcHnOGaIH6wtRZ-0|smart-link]

steps.action:
WHEN a user is on the Home page

AND Game content is displayed in the Hero/Featured space
steps.result:
THEN the featured game image is displayed on the right 2/3 of the screen

AND the featured game image has a white border around the image indicating focus

AND the featured game image does not have a gradient applied

AND the featured game details are displayed to the left of the featured image, below the navigation header buttons (See attached screenshots and Figma designs for reference)

summary: No Broadcast Available
description:
GIVEN an entitled user is logged in
AND a Spring Training game with no broadcasters assigned is present in the Hero/Featured spot on the Home page

_NOTE:_ Easiest way to set this up is to login with a TMobile or Extra Innings user, and set the user’s favorite team to a team in a game with no broadcasters. Forge content may need to be adjusted.

steps.action: WHEN a user selects the featured image for a game with no broadcasts assigned
steps.result: THEN a modal is displayed with text "No Broadcast Available"

summary: Today's Lineup Carousel Animation
description:
GIVEN a yearly subscriber is logged in
AND there are games scheduled on today's date
AND featured forge content is published
AND the user is on the Home page

*NOTE:* Figma designs for reference - [https://www.figma.com/design/rGAWoDJL0fx7RTxv0er9U5/MLB.TV-Connected-Device-Developer-Spec?node-id=241-103414&m=dev|https://www.figma.com/design/rGAWoDJL0fx7RTxv0er9U5/MLB.TV-Connected-Device-Developer-Spec?node-id=241-103414&m=dev|smart-link]

steps.action:
WHEN the user navigates to the "Today’s Lineup" carousel
AND the user navigates away from the "Today’s Lineup" carousel
steps.result:
THEN all the tiles in the carousel transition from mini tiles to expanded tiles once focus is assigned to the carousel
AND the carousels below the Today’s Lineup carousel are moved down to make room for the expanded tiles in the Today’s Lineup
AND all the tiles in the carousel transition from expanded tiles to mini tiles once focus moves away from the carousel
AND the carousels below the Today’s Lineup carousel are moved back up once the Today’s Lineup tiles transition back to mini tiles

steps.action:

AND presses the "back" button on the remote controller twice
steps.result:

AND focus is assigned to the "Log Out" button

summary: Bookmarked carousel position navigating away from RSN page
description:
GIVEN a user is on a RSN page
AND focus is present in a carousel
AND focus is not assigned to the first item in a carousel

steps.action:
WHEN the user navigates away from the RSN page
AND the user navigates back to the same RSN page
steps.result: THEN the previously focused carousel has been reset to its original order

summary: RSN Carousel navigation to the right
description:
GIVEN a user is on a RSN page
AND focus is assigned to a carousel

steps.action: WHEN the user presses the right arrow on the controller
steps.result:
THEN focus remains on the furthest left full tile
AND the tile that was previously to the right of the focused tile is now in the focused position of the carousel
AND focus remains on the tile once the user reaches the last tile in the carousel
AND the user can no longer continue navigating right through the carousel once the end of the carousel has been reached

summary: Publica API - Failed calls during stream playback does not result in video error - Live game
description:
GIVEN an International user is logged in
AND the user selects a Live in-progress game for playback

steps.action:
WHEN the user has initiated playback of a Live game
AND an ad break occurs
AND network calls to "[pbs.getpublicac.om|http://pbs.getpublicac.om]" are blocked
OR network calls to "[pbs.getpublica.com|http://pbs.getpublica.com]" are failing
steps.result:
THEN the stream continues playback with no video error modal popping up
AND ads playback during the ad break

summary: Game Tile - Badges - Split Squad Logic

steps.action: WHEN user navigates to a date with teams who are splitting their squad to play more than one game (eg Spring Training: Monday, March 2, 2026)
steps.data: splitSquad: true in the EPG response
steps.result: THEN the game tile will display a white badge, showing {{<TEAM_NAME> SPLIT SQUAD}} if only one team is splitting their squad that day or {{SPLIT SQUAD GAME}} if both teams are spitting their teams that day.

summary: Game Scrubber - Match Up
description: GIVEN a logged in user is entitled for archive game playback

steps.action: WHEN the user selects an archive game/feed for playback
steps.result:
THEN the video player is displayed
AND the team logos and city abbreviations (ATL, CIN) are displayed left justified above the scrubber bar
AND the logos display in order of Away team, Home team

summary: Games page background
description:

NOTE: [https://www.figma.com/design/rGAWoDJL0fx7RTxv0er9U5/MLB.TV-Connected-Device-Developer-Spec?node-id=421-74460&m=dev|https://www.figma.com/design/rGAWoDJL0fx7RTxv0er9U5/MLB.TV-Connected-Device-Developer-Spec?node-id=421-74460&m=dev|smart-link]

steps.action:

AND video playback resumes after the second button press
AND a play flash icon is displayed for one second before it disappears

summary: MASN2 24/7 feed Jump to Live
description:
GIVEN a user is entitled for MASN2 RSN
AND the user selects the MASN2 24/7 stream to watch
AND the playhead is behind the live point

steps.action:
WHEN the scrubber is displayed
AND the user selects the "Jump to Live" button
steps.result:
THEN playback moves to the live point
AND the playhead is all the way to the right on the scrubber
AND the entire scrubber bar is blue
AND a red Live indicator is displayed
AND the "Jump to Live" button does not display
AND the elapsed time in the scrubber reflects the playhead position

summary: HDR Feature Flag set to ON
description:
GIVEN the {{hasHDR}} {{featureFlags}} is set to {{true}} in the config

*NOTE:* The configs can be seen via the Network tab using Dev Tools in Chrome. Configs will come through as {{dev.json}}, {{qa.json}}, {{stage.json}}, {{mock.json}}, {{prod.json}} depending on the environment the build is pointing to. You can view the configs by selecting the {{.json}} then selecting the Preview tab. Follow path {{featureFlags}} → {{video}} → {{hasHDR}} → {{enabled}}.

steps.action:

steps.result: THEN the HDR toggle setting is displayed on the Settings page

summary: Basic RSN Forge tiles - focused
description:
GIVEN the user has RSN entitlements
AND the user is logged in

*NOTE:* RSN user examples: MASN/MASN2, SNLA+, SNY

steps.action: WHEN a RSN user views a focused VOD basic tile on an RSN page
steps.result:
THEN the tile has a white border to indicate focus
AND the title section of the tile is white to indicate focus
AND the associated thumbnail displays
AND the duration of the VOD content is displayed in the lower right corner of the thumbnail

AND the video published date is displayed left justified below the title

summary: bufferHealth metadata in DataDog
description:
GIVEN a tester has logged into the Core-App with a test user
AND the tester opens this DataDog heuristics [<link>|https://us5.datadoghq.com/logs?query=service%3Aconnected-devices---mlb.tv---webstream%20%40usr.email%3Adm5tv%40gmail.com&agg_m=%40event.asset.fguid&agg_m_source=base&agg_q=%40model&agg_q_source=base&agg_t=cardinality&analyticsOptions=%5B%22line%22%2C%22dog_classic%22%2Cnull%2Cnull%2C%22value%22%5D&clustering_pattern_field_path=message&cols=host%2Cservice%2C%40usr.id%2C%40event.action%2C%40convivaTimestamp%2C%40event.reason%2C%40event.metadata.currentLevel%2C%40modelYear&context_event=AY_CgMDtAAD8tvFqYXPtGgAB&fromUser=true&messageDisplay=inline&refresh_mode=sliding&storage=hot&stream_sort=time%2Cdesc&top_n=10&top_o=top&view=spans&viz=stream&x_missing=true&from_ts=1752684037199&to_ts=1752687637199&live=true]

NOTE: 

* In DataDog, @user.email value will need to be updated to the logged in test user

See this comment/ticket for more information: [https://baseball.atlassian.net/browse/COW-1087|https://baseball.atlassian.net/browse/COW-1087|smart-link]

steps.action:
WHEN the user selects content in the Core-App for playback
AND the user initiates FF and RW to induce buffering
AND the tester selects the log for {{EVENT.ACTION player_rebuffering}} in DataDog
steps.data:
Content Types

* Live Game
* Archive Game
* SVOD
* VOD
* SLIVE
* FLIVE
* MLBN 24/7 linear feed
* RSN 24/7 linear feed
steps.result: THEN values should be present in this path: {{metadata}} -> {{bufferHealth}}

summary: Media Recovery Buffer Stall - DataDog
description:
GIVEN a user that is entitled for video playback is logged in
AND the tester has opened the below DataDog link (be sure to update the search filter for the logged in test user)

**NOTE:** 
Test via the Core App URL in Chrome
Playback Heuristics URL: [https://us5.datadoghq.com/logs?query=%22PLAYBACK%20HEURISTICS%22%20%40usr.id%3A00uhs4yw8qjJKy4xb357&agg_m=%40event.metadata.currentBitrate&agg_m_source=base&agg_q=%40asset.isLive&agg_q_source=base&agg_t=avg&analyticsOptions=%5B%22line%22%2C%22dog_classic%22%2Cnull%2Cnull%5D&cols=host%2Cservice%2C%40usr.id%2C%40event.action%2C%40event.reason%2C%40event.metadata.convivaTimestamp&context_event=AY_CgMDtAAD8tvFqYXPtGgAB&fromUser=true&messageDisplay=inline&refresh_mode=sliding&storage=hot&stream_sort=%40convivaTimestamp%2Casc&top_n=10&top_o=top&view=spans&viz=&x_missing=true&from_ts=1726669870274&to_ts=1726684270274&live=true|https://us5.datadoghq.com/logs?query=%22PLAYBACK%20HEURISTICS%22%20%40usr.id%3A00uhs4yw8qjJKy4xb357&agg_m=%40event.metadata.currentBitrate&agg_m_source=base&agg_q=%40asset.isLive&agg_q_source=base&agg_t=avg&analyticsOptions=%5B%22line%22%2C%22dog_classic%22%2Cnull%2Cnull%5D&cols=host%2Cservice%2C%40usr.id%2C%40event.action%2C%40event.reason%2C%40event.metadata.convivaTimestamp&context_event=AY_CgMDtAAD8tvFqYXPtGgAB&fromUser=true&messageDisplay=inline&refresh_mode=sliding&storage=hot&stream_sort=%40convivaTimestamp%2Casc&top_n=10&top_o=top&view=spans&viz=&x_missing=true&from_ts=1726669870274&to_ts=1726684270274&live=true]

steps.action:

summary: Game Tile - Badging Priority & Rules (Post UI Refresh)
description:
GIVEN a logged in user is on the Games or Home page

# The Badge Priority should be as follows:
## Live
## Pre/Postgame shows
## Entitlement Gated:
### BLACKOUT
### GEICO FREE GAME
### MVPD REQUIRED
### LOCKED
## Interruption
### Delayed, Suspended, Cancelled, etc.,
## Special State
### PERFECT
### NO HITTER
### etc.,

Rules:

* In mini-state only show two badges. Game State (Red badges: Live, pregame show, etc) and User status  (White badges: Locked, MVPD Required, Time, Split Squad)
* In the larger state it should go Game states (red badges), user state (white badges), blue (geico, perfect, no hitter etc)
* If the game is MVPD Required, it should always show “MVPD Required” over “Locked.” We do not want to show “Locked” just for someone to then buy a package and see they need to MVPD auth anyway.
* When break glass mode is turned on (no blackouts, entitled: yes) - no locked badges should be displayed on game card, even when no tv broadcasts exist.

steps.action: WHEN a user views a game tile in a focused or unfocused state
steps.result: THEN the badges in the upper left corner of the screen follow the rules and priority outlined in the description

steps.data:
{{content}} = 

* Live / Archive game (network calls: tv-fst.mlb.com or tv-gmc.mlb.com)
* MLBN / SLIVE (network calls: tve-live.mlb.com)
* SVOD / VOD (network calls: mlb-cuts-diamond.mlb.com)
* RSN 24/7 (network calls: tve-live.mlb.com)
* RSN VOD (network calls: mlb-cuts-diamond.mlb.com)

summary: Publica requests stop after exiting a stream
description: GIVEN a user is entitled to watch a live video stream

steps.action: WHEN a user exits a stream
steps.result: THEN network calls to "[pbs.getpublica.com|http://pbs.getpublica.com]" are no longer occuring

summary: Live Game state "NO HITTER"
description:
GIVEN a subscriber is on the Games or Home page
AND Hide Spoilers is set to OFF

steps.action: WHEN a live game has a pitcher who has not allowed a hit to the opposing team and the game has reached the 6th inning - then statsAPI will send a statusCode value of ???
steps.result: THEN a Blue ‘NO HITTER’ tag is presented to user on Hero and Expanded Game Tile (Not present in mini)

summary: Live Game state "PERFECT"
description:
GIVEN a subscriber is on the Games or Home page
AND Hide Spoilers is set to OFF

steps.action: WHEN a live game has a pitcher who has not allowed any opposing batter to reach base for any reason (hit/walk/error) and the game has reached the 6th inning
steps.result: THEN a Blue ‘PERFECT GAME’ badge is displayed on the game badge (expanded & hero only) - not present in mini state

summary: MASN2 24/7 feed Pause/Play
description:
GIVEN a logged in user is entitled for MASN2 RSN
AND the user is playing back the MASN2 24/7 stream

steps.action:
WHEN the user presses the "select" button on the controller
AND the user presses the "select" button again after a a minute
steps.result:
THEN video playback pauses after the first button press
AND video playback resumes after the second button press
AND the elapsed time in the scrubber reflects the playhead position
AND the live indicator is grey, indicating not at the live point
AND the "Jump to Live" button displays right justified above the scrubber bar

summary: MASN2 VOD RW Stop
description:
GIVEN a logged in user is entitled for MASN2 RSN
AND the user is on the MASN2 page
AND the user selects a VOD to watch

steps.action:
WHEN the user initiates Rewind
AND presses the "select" button on the controller after a few moments
steps.result:
THEN Rewind stops
AND playback resumes at the point where Rewind stopped
AND the elapsed time in the scrubber reflects the playhead position

summary: "BACK" button navigates user to the top of the RSN page
description:
Given user is navigating the MLB app 
And user is on a RSN page

steps.action:
WHEN focus is not on the RSN featured image
OR focus is on a RSN forge tile
AND the user select the "Back" button on their remote control
steps.result:
THEN the user is redirected to the top of the page - w/ focus on the RSN nav button
AND all carousels will be reset to their original state.

summary: MASN+ page load
description:
GIVEN a user has entitlements for MASN+
AND the user launches the MLB.tv Core app

*NOTE:* 
MASN+ entitlement = MASN_110

MASN Test users: [https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/15fajYxYv0k04eO4aKGtXxWVPABEvgaK26Jt5OoQBC6Y/edit?usp=sharing|https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/15fajYxYv0k04eO4aKGtXxWVPABEvgaK26Jt5OoQBC6Y/edit?usp=sharing|smart-link]

steps.action: WHEN the user selects the [MASN] tab in the header
steps.result:
THEN the MASN+ page loads successfully
AND MASN+ content is present in the Hero
AND a title is present in the Hero
AND a description is present in the Hero
AND a MASN related background image is present in the Featured Image space
AND text "PRESENTED BY" is displayed below the Hero content description
AND the presented by Logo is displayed below the "PRESENTED BY" text
AND MASN related carousels are displayed
AND VOD cards are displayed in the carousels for available VOD content

**NOTE:** "Presented By" and logo are feature flagged and may not display based on the set value. This can be checked in one of the following (based on environment) "base.json", "dev.json", "qa.json", "prod.json". Path: rsn -> masn -> enabled

summary: MASN and MASN2 24/7 feed - Mid-roll DAI Ads
description:
GIVEN a user has entitlements for MASN
AND the user launches the MLB.tv Core App
AND the user logs in
AND the user has initiated playback of the 24/7 MASN feeds
AND the 24/7 MASN streams have adsEnabled equals true in the graphql Preview/Response (data->initPlaybackSession->adExperience->adsEnabled)
AND an asset key is is returned in the same graphql Preview/Response (data->initPlaybackSession->adExperience->adEngineIdentifiers)

*NOTE:* 
MASN entitlement = MASN_110 and MASN_120
Needs to be tested in a browser

|masnims@gmail.com (In Market)|Password4MASN|
|masnoom@gmail.com (Out of Market)|Password4MASN|
|masnintl@gmail.com (International)|Password4MASN|

AND the "id3-events.json" network calls have a request URL starting with "[https://dai.google.com/|https://dai.google.com/]"

steps.result: THEN parameter {{is_lat}} is passed for users based upon {{lmt}} API value from Comcast (true / false)

summary: N/A - R5100B - The total save data doesn't exceed 1GB
description:
**NOTE:** The PS5 app doesn't use saved data on the kit.

**Save Data/Download  Data**

**R5100 Requirement:** The total save data size used by the application is 1 GiB or less per user

**Description**
The backup data size for save data and size of the save data system area are not included in the save data size.
Refer to the SaveData Library Overview document regarding size of the save data system area.

steps.action:
1. Select "Application" on the home screen
2. Create all save data that can be created by the application
3. Go back to the home screen, then press the up button
4. Select "Settings" > "Saved Data and Games/Apps Settings" > "Saved Data (PS5)" > "Console Storage" > "Delete" > the applicable application and press options button
5. Check the value of XXXX of "★Size XXXXMB" in "★Information"
steps.result: The total save data size per user is "1.08 GB" or less.

summary: DAI API - Failed calls during stream playback does not result in a video error - Archive game
description:
GIVEN an Archive game stream has adsEnabled equals true in the graphql Preview/Response (data->initPlaybackSession->adExperience->adsEnabled)
AND a cmsId and a videoId is returned in the same graphql Preview/Response (data->initPlaybackSession->adExperience->adEngineIdentifiers)

*NOTE:*
Enter graphql into the Network search of Dev Tools to find the initPlaybackSession

steps.action:
WHEN the user initiates playback of an Archive game
AND network calls to "[dai.google.com|http://dai.google.com]*" are blocked
OR network calls to "[dai.google.com|http://dai.google.com]*" are failing
steps.result: THEN the stream continues to playback with no video error modal popping up

summary: R5023A - Application runs after app version is updated
description:
**Compatibility**

**R5023 Requirement:** The application runs correctly in all possible combinations and orders of previously QA approved packages and the package to be submitted

**Description:** Before submitting the current package, check that the application runs correctly in all possible combinations of previously QA passed application packages and additional contents.

steps.action:
1. Place the cursor on the "Application" on the home screen
2. Press the options button and select "★Check"
3. Confirm the value of "param.json Content Version = " is larger than 01.000.000 in [Log #000 (original app)]
4. Go back to the home screen, then select the "Application"
5. Check throughout the entire application
steps.result: The application runs correctly when installing an application package with an application version (contentVersion) greater than 01.000.000 without prior installation of the 01.000.000 version package.

summary: R5071A - The shake test
description:
**Peripheral Devices**

**R5071 Requirement:**
The application does not instruct or ask the user to vigorously shake the controller with one hand.

steps.action:
1. Select the "Application" on the home screen
2. Check throughout the entire application

**Notes:**
Pay particular attention when motion sensor features are being used.
steps.result: Users are not requested or instructed to vigorously shake the wireless controller for PlayStation®5 with one hand.

summary: N/A - R5096F - The application does not crash when there is insufficient free space for save data
description:
**NOTE:** The PS5 app doesn't use saved data on the kit.

**Save Data/Download Data**

**R5096 Requirement:** When there is insufficient space to create or update save data, the following are fulfilled.
-- Allow the user to retry the saving process
-- The user is not required to restart the application to enable saving
-- The application calls sceSaveDataDialogOpen() using the values specified in the table in the Description section below

**Description**
**Cannot be saved**
If saving could not be performed due to insufficient space or another reason, allow the user to retry the saving process. The application can either allow the user to retry immediately or delay retrying until the user progresses to the next saving point. (Infinite loops, aside from situations related to insufficient space, are not recommended)

Saving must not be disabled throughout the application after the process has been interrupted; whether the application utilizes an autosave feature or a user initiated save feature.

If an error occurs during saving of the save data, it is possible to display the type of error to the user by using the error code display mode of Save Data Dialog.

**Cannot save due to insufficient free space**
When there is insufficient free space to mount save data directories, sceSaveDataMount3() will return SCE_SAVE_DATA_ERROR_NO_SPACE_FS. When this occurs, the sceSaveDataDialogOpen() needs to be called as detailed below:

|||Arguments|Value to specify
||SceSaveDataDialogParam.mode|SCE_SAVE_DATA_DIALOG_MODE_SYSTEM_MSG
||SceSaveDataDialogParam.dispType|SCE_SAVE_DATA_DIALOG_TYPE_SAVE
||SceSaveDataDialogParam.sysMsgParam->sysMsgType|SCE_SAVE_DATA_DIALOG_SYSMSG_TYPE_NOSPACE or SCE_SAVE_DATA_DIALOG_SYSMSG_TYPE_NOSPACE_CONTINUABLE

After displaying the system defined message, when free space is not available, the application can either allow game progress, or block it until the user frees the necessary space.

Save data memory processing is not relevant to this requirement. However, for applications that use both save data memory and normal save data, ensure the normal save data complies with this requirement because insufficient free space is occurred for the normal save data.

steps.action:
1. Press the up button on the home screen
2. Select "Settings" > "Saved Data and Game/Apps Settings" > "Saved Data (PS5)" > "Console Storage" > "Delete", and check that the save data for the corresponding application has not been created
3. Return to the home screen and set "★Debug Settings" > "Game" > "SaveData" > "Fake Free Space" to "On"
4. Go back to the home screen, then select the "Application"
5. Proceed to a save point and attempt to create save data

**Notes**
Also check when overwriting existing save data.
It is permissible to display a system defined message until insufficient free space is resolved.
steps.result:
1. If a save fails due to insufficient free space, the application recovers to a state where the save can be attempted again without losing any progress information.
2. When save data mounting fails due to insufficient free space, one of the following system messages is displayed.

[T-003][Info]
SaveDataDialog:
System Message - NOSPACE

[T-003][Info]
SaveDataDialog:
System Message - NOSPACE_CONTINUABLE

summary: R5026A - Verify SIE-licensed peripherals work as expected
description:
**Peripheral Devices**

**R5026 Requirement:**
The application runs correctly when any supported SIE-licensed peripheral is:
- already connected on boot
- connected or disconnected while the application is running

**Description:**
Connecting or disconnecting SIE-licensed peripherals that are supported by the application must not cause the application to malfunction.

If a non-SIE-licensed peripheral (the use of which has been previously approved by SIE) is required, SIE may request provision of the peripheral upon master submission.

**QA NOTE:**
Peripherals to be tested by GFQA are as follows. Only peripherals that are supported by the application are used for testing.
- Wireless controller for PlayStation®5

steps.action:
For each peripheral device:
1. Connect the peripheral
2. Select the "Application" on the home screen
3. Proceed to where the peripheral will be used and use it
4. Disconnect the peripheral
5. Reconnect the peripheral

**Notes**
Perform this test at various points in each application mode, the options screen, and any screens that display messages.
steps.result:
The peripheral to be tested can be used.
When connecting and disconnecting the peripheral, the application does not freeze or otherwise become inoperable.

summary: R5037A - No references are made to Sony or Playstation

**Intellectual Property**

**R5037 Requirement:** The four-letter SONY logo, PlayStation® related logos, and the SIE corporate logo are not used in the application except in the following cases.
- Line drawing, newly-created illustration, or photo image provided by SIE is used pursuant to R5038
- The SIE trademark notices are displayed pursuant to R5039
- The combination of the PlayStation™Store logo and the PlayStation™Store icon is displayed pursuant to R5051
- Promotional videos related to PlayStation® platform titles

**Note:** The MLB app makes no references to, or mentions Sony/Playstation in any form or fashion.

steps.action:

2. Check throughout the entire application
steps.result:

The four-letter SONY logo and PlayStation® related logos are not used on anything except SIE-provided line drawings of systems/peripherals, photo images, and when displaying the PlayStation™Store icon.

summary: R5033J - The application does not freeze or become inoperable when a network error occurs
description:
**Network**

**R5033 Premise:** If network features are used

**R5033 Requirement:** The application does not freeze, user cancel operations are not blocked, and the application does not otherwise become inoperable from the effects of communication errors or another client's actions.

steps.action:
1. Sign into PlayStation™Network
2. Disconnect from the network to cause a network error
3. Select the "Application" on the home screen
4. Proceed to a point that uses the network features and attempt to start the network features

**Notes:**
- Check at all points where network features are used. This includes non-gameplay features such as leaderboards, SNS features, where information is displayed only when connected to a network (for example, news ticker, update information) etc.
- For methods to disconnect from the network in step (2), refer to the following.
---- When connected to a Wi-Fi network, turn off the power of the wireless router or unplug the WAN-side LAN cable from the wireless router.
---- When using a wired LAN connection, remove the LAN cable connected to the PlayStation®5 console.
---- Select "Settings" on the System View > "Network" > "Settings" and disable "Connect to the Internet".
---- Select "Settings" on the System View > "Users and Accounts" > "Other" > "Sign Out".
steps.result: The application does not freeze or otherwise become inoperable when attempting to use network features in a state where there is a network error

summary: R5065A - Application supports HD resolutions
description:
**Graphical User Interface**

**R5065 Requirement:** In HD resolution, confirm that visibility required to use the application and play the game is maintained. Moreover, check that application progress is possible regardless of the video output format. Also confirm the visibility at HD resolutions for the essential items described in R5030.

steps.action:
1. Press the up button on the home screen.
2. Confirm that "Settings" > "Screen and Video" > "Video Output" > "Resolution" is set to one of the resolutions to check ("720p", "1080i", "1080p", or "2160p).
3. Go back to the home screen and select the "Application".
4. Check throughout the entire application.

**Notes:**
- Check with all resolutions in step (4), however when testing 1080 only test "1080i" or "1080p".
- In particular, be sure to check full motion video/full motion animation.
steps.result:
- The application can be started in all supported HD resolutions.
- Visibility necessary to use the application and perform gameplay is maintained throughout.

summary: Reboot
description: GIVEN

steps.action:
Execute app → Terminate app → Reboot app

[CASE0 - OS]
1. NetCast
- App launching screen, My Apps, Live TV, HOME Dashboard, Search

2. webOS
- When app is terminated: Launcher Bar, App launching screen, Search
- When app remains on Recent List: Recent List, Launcher Bar, Search

[CASE1 - Remote/HW Key]
1. Execute app → Turn TV power off using Remote/HW Key → TV Power On → Execute app → check movement
2. Execute app → play video content → Turn TV power off using Remote/HW Key → TV Power On → Execute app → Check video content playback

[CASE2 - Plug]
1. Execute app → Unplug → Plugin → TV Power on → Execute app → Check movement
2. Execute app → Play video content → Unplug → Plugin → TV Power on → Execute app → Check movement
steps.result:
When rebooting app, playback and movement is proper without any error

※ Recent List
webOS: App does not remain on Recent List when rebooting TV (both AC/DC)
webOS2.0: AC Off > Initialize Recent List / DC Off: Remains on Recent List 
webOS3.0: App remains on Recent List when rebooting TV (both AC and DC)

※ Terminate app using EXIT UI Button
NetCast: Move to Live TV of My App 
webOS/webOS2.0: When user moves to Live TV, app should be remaining on Recent List
webOS3.0: When user moves to Live TV, app does not have to remain on Recent List (a concept for memory management)  

※ Video contents should be divided as free/paid contents, and all of them should be checked

※ webOS 
- Should check all ways of execution (both from Recent List and Launcher Bar) 

※ QSM+ supporting platform: Both QSM+ On/Off settings should be checked

summary: R5030B - Essential items are displayed within the adjusted display area
description:
**Graphical User Interface**

**R5030 Premise:** If the application disables the "Scaling to the Display's Safe Area"

**R5030 Requirement:** Essential items are displayed using one of the following methods:
- Design the screen so that essential items are included approximately in the center 90% of the vertical/horizontal measurements (90% x 90% of the area)
- Essential items are included in the safe area that can be obtained with sceSystemServiceGetDisplaySafeAreaInfo().

**Description**
For details on setting the "Scaling to the Display's Safe Area", refer to Param.json File Specification.
"Essential items" are as follows:
- Error messages and warning messages defined by the TRC
- Legal texts such as the EULA and other copyright indications
- Online IDs used as the identifiers of users

In order to display long strings of characters the application can implement a scrolling feature; it is also acceptable to include features such as "See Details" to have the strings displayed in a separate screen.

It is prohibited to provide a proprietary screen adjustment feature that overlaps with the "Adjust Display Area" feature in the application. Because features to adjust UI parts or HUD elements and a feature to change the aspect ratio of the screen do not overlap with the "Adjust Display Area" feature, a proprietary screen adjustment feature can be provided in the application.

**QA NOTE:** Check in all languages supported by the application.

steps.action:
1. Press the up button on the home screen
2. Set "Settings" > "Screen and Video" > "Screen" > "Adjust Display Area" to an arbitrary value
3. Go back to the home screen, then select the "Application"
4. Proceed to where an essential item is displayed
5. Close the "Application"
6. Press the up button on the home screen
7. Set "Settings" > "Screen and Video" > "Screen" > "Adjust Display Area" to a setting that differs from step (2)
8. Go back to the home screen, then select the "Application"
9. Proceed to where the essential item confirmed in step (4) is displayed

**Notes:** Check with all resolutions in step (4).
steps.result:
When displaying essential items according to the display area settings set in the system software
- Essential items are displayed within the display area set by "Adjust Display Area".
- The application does not provide its own screen adjustment feature to adjust the overall screen.
- When displaying long character strings, the display is scrolled or a "See details" feature displays the content on a separate screen.

summary: R5018A - Display an animation if the required loading time exceeds 30 seconds.
description:
**Graphical User Interface**

**R5018 Requirement:** If the time required for scene transition (such as entering into a certain game mode or switching chapter/stage/level) exceeds 30 seconds, the application displays an animation. Progress information such as a progress bar or the remaining time is displayed if the time required for scene transition exceeds 60 seconds.

**Description**
If the time required for scene transition exceeds 30 seconds, an animation must be displayed. Progress information must be displayed if the time required for scene transition exceeds 60 seconds so that it is not deemed as a "suspended unresponsive screen state" as defined in R5019.

Recommended implementations for displaying progress information are:
- Repeatedly flashing a message that explains what is being processed (ex. Now downloading required assets, Now waiting for other players, etc.)"
- Repeatedly moving an icon up and down

It is not recommended to use vague terms to explain scene transitions, especially, please refrain from using "loading" since no-loading user experience is one of the vision SIE would like to achieve with PlayStation®5 console.

Examples of situations covered by this requirement that may prolong the time required for scene transitions are:
- Downloading of files to download
- Network activity that cannot be cancelled by the user e.g.
-- Waiting for other players over the network
-- Waiting on server response for score ranking, matching, etc

Network related waiting times are also covered in R5033.

Downloading package files for PlayGo scenarios is exempt from this requirement. However, if the files required for game progress are not completely downloaded and game progress becomes impossible, it is strongly recommended to display progress information to users, similar to this requirement.

**QA NOTE**
- For this test case, please set "★Debug Settings" >"Game" > "Slow SSD Mode" to "Off".
- Check in all instances where scene transition occurs, such as when the application is launching and when changing chapters, stages, or modes.
- Icons and text for scene transitions that do not move or change violate this requirement. Acceptable animations include scrolling the text or changing its color and moving or rotating icons.
- Please perform this test case after the application starts up and processing on the application-side is started. Application startup can be confirmed with the following system message.
[T-004][Info]
SystemSoftware:
Application Started.

steps.action:
1. Select the "Application" on the home screen
2. Proceed to where scene transition is performed
steps.result:
The application displays an animation if the time required for scene transition exceeds 30 seconds.

Progress information such as a progress bar or the remaining time is displayed if the time required for scene transition exceeds 60 seconds.

summary: R5034A - Display a message when a network error occurs
description:
**Network**

**R5034 Premise:** If network features are used

**R5034 Requirement:** When a network error occurs, the application displays a message appropriate to the network error that occurred.

**Description**
When a network error occurs, notify the user with a message indicating the occurrence of the error such as, "A network error occurred". In addition to the error notification, it is recommended that the user is notified of the cause of the error and how to address the error. The appropriateness of this information is outside the scope of the requirement, however, it will be a violation of this requirement if a clearly incorrect message is displayed - for example, a message describing the cause of the error to be restrictions of social features when the real cause is a network disconnection.

This requirement excludes situations when a network error occurs upon an application suspend/resume, but the network communication can be restored and game progress can be resumed immediately.

If there is a game interruption due to an error code generated from the system or library, calling the error dialog is recommended. The appropriate message and/or error handling will be displayed according to the error code.

**QA NOTE**
- Check at all points where the application uses network features.
- This requirement only applies when a communication error occurs during usage of network features and not when starting network features while disconnected from the network.

steps.action:
1. Select the "Application" on the home screen
2. Proceed to a point that uses network features
3. Disconnect from the network to cause a communication error

**Notes:**
Confirm by disconnecting from the network with the following methods.
- When connected to a Wi-Fi network, turn off the power of the wireless router or unplug the WAN-side LAN cable from the wireless router.
- When using a wired LAN connection, remove the LAN cable connected to the PlayStation®5 console.
- Press and hold the PS button to return to the home screen > press the up button > select "Settings" > "Network" > "Settings" and disable "Connect to the Internet".
steps.result: When a communication error occurs, a message is displayed stating that a network error occurred

summary: R5019A - Suspended unresponsive screen states do not last longer than 30 seconds
description:
**Graphical User Interface**

**R5019 Requirement:**  Confirm that suspended unresponsive screen states do not last longer than 30 seconds.

"Suspended unresponsive screen states" are states where both of the following conditions are met.
- The screen display does not change
- There are no responses to user operations

On the other hand, the following are not included in "suspended unresponsive screen states".
- When the screen does not change but the user is prompted to perform an operation to transition to another screen, such as in scenes where a "Press a button" text message is displayed or an audio instruction is given
- When user operations are not accepted while the screen continually changes, such as scene transitions which are defined in R5018, scenes where a movie clip is being played back in the middle of game progress as part of the game presentation, or character dialogue scenes

**Description**
This rule is set so that users will not mistake a suspended unresponsive screen that lasts for a long time as a freeze. It is recommended to use animation in accordance with R5018 even if a suspended screen lasts less than 30 seconds. It is possible to use animation in accordance with R5018 or accept some user operations in order to prevent suspended unresponsive screen states.

Also refer to Description for R5033 when implementing processing that waits for a response from a server for score ranking, matching, etc.

**QA NOTE**
- For this test case, please set "★Debug Settings" >"Game" > "Slow SSD Mode" to "Off".
- Check all locations in the application where a "suspended and unresponsive screen" continues, including online areas.
- Please perform this test case after the application starts up and processing on the application-side is started. Application startup can be confirmed with the following system message.
[T-004][Info]
SystemSoftware:
Application Started.

steps.action:
1. Select the "Application" on the home screen
2. Proceed to where an "suspended and unresponsive screen" continues
steps.result: The application does not display "suspended and unresponsive screens" for longer than 30 seconds.

summary: R5162A - Audio is played back for different Audio Output Format settings
description:
**Audio**

**R5162 Requirement:** The application’s audio output works correctly even if the system software audio output format and/or audio output attributes are modified.

steps.action:
1. To confirm "Audio Output Format", on the home screen, set "★Debug Settings" > "Sound and Screen" > "Audio Output Format" to "LPCM 2ch"
2. Return to the home screen
3. Select the "Application" and check that the application's audio is played
4. Close the "Application"
5. Repeat steps (1) to (4) for each of the following settings
- "LPCM 5.1ch"
- "LPCM 7.1ch"
- "LPCM 2ch (TV Virtual Surround)"
6. To confirm headphones, on the home screen, set "★Debug Settings" > "Sound and Screen" > "Audio Output Format" to "Auto"
7. Connect headphones to the headphone jack of the wireless controller for PlayStation®5
8. Return to the home screen, set "Settings" > "Sound" > "Audio Output" > "Output to Headphones" to "All Audio" and enable "Enable 3D Audio for Headphones"
9. Return to the home screen
10. Select the "Application" and check that the application's audio is played

**Notes:**
Regardless of application support/lack of support, confirm that audio is played in all audio output formats (2ch/5.1ch/7.1ch/2ch(TV Virtual Surround)) and when headphones are connected.
steps.result:
Audio is played back regardless of the "Audio Output Format" setting
Audio is played back even when headphones are connected

summary: R5017B - Application functions when waking from Rest Mode
description:
**Suspend/Resume feature**

**R5017 Requirement:**  The features implemented in the application have been confirmed to run as intended with a Testing Kit with "Release Check Mode" set to "Release Mode".

**Description:**  Since Development Kits are designed to prioritize convenience for development, some of the system behavior may vary from retail units. By using Testing Kits with "Release Check Mode" set to "Release Mode" to test application behavior, it will be possible to test the operation as intended in retail unit environments. Be sure to test the application with Testing Kits before submitting the master to SIE.

Note that operation testing that assumes a retail unit environment will not be possible with a Testing Kit set to "Assist Mode", therefore it will not be possible to use it for operation testing of this requirement.

steps.action:
1. Select the "Application"
2. Advance to an arbitrary point in the application
3. Press and release the PS button > press the down button on the Control Center > select "Power" > "Enter Rest Mode"
4. Leave the system in rest mode for 6 to 9 hours
5. Press the PS button on the wireless controller for PlayStation®5 to wake the system from rest mode
6. Check throughout the entire application

**Notes:**
- When the PS button is pressed during mid-action (e.g. when a character is mid-fall) if the application resumes gameplay at the same point when the PS button is pressed again then the same should also occur at that point when resuming from Rest Mode when performing steps (3) to (5).
- Perform step (3) throughout the application and especially check the following areas.
---- Just prior to an event occurring or an item being obtained
---- During use of network features. Note, when resuming from rest mode in a network feature a network disconnection will occur
steps.result:
When the application wakes from the rest mode, the following occurs.

The application does not freeze or otherwise become inoperable.
All areas and game modes (e.g. menus) are displayed and are accessible.

steps.result: When executing app, lockup or latch, device power turns off

summary: Factory Reset
description: GIVEN

steps.action: Reset device to factory settings → Execute app → Playback contents
steps.result: able to access and execute app properly

summary: Resume archive playback Skip through inning markers - Hide Spoilers OFF
description:
GIVEN the user is entitle for archive game playback
AND Hide Spoilers is turned OFF
AND the archive game contains a resume bookmark anytime after the top of the 1st inning

steps.action:
WHEN the user resumes archive game playback
AND the user opens the scrubber/player controls
AND the user selects each top and bottom inning in the line score
steps.result:
THEN a tile with text and thumbnails display for each milestone
AND the playback head jumps to the appropriate inning marker
AND the game playback point matches the inning marker

summary: Play Contents/Replay
description: GIVEN

steps.action: Start App → Enter random series playback content → Complete playback → Playback content again
steps.result: After finishing playback of a content, it is possible to replay content

summary: HOME Key
description: GIVEN

steps.action: Execute app → Press HOME key of remote control
steps.result:
HOME key of remote control works properly

※ NetCast: App should move to Home dashboard after terminating
※ webOS: Home Launcher Bar should appear

summary: Play Contents/Playback Control/MMRC
description: Uses developer company's own API

steps.action:
Execute app → Access random video content → Play content → Check Playback Control movement 

[CASE]
1. Each UI button works properly - refer to UX scenario for movement details
2. Each remote control key mapped in UI button works properly
3. UI button and remote key works identically
steps.result:
- Check if playback control function is working properly
   ->  Check Play, Pause, Stop, Fast Forward, Rewind, Seek, 
        Previous, Next, button
   -> Check Playback Control operation with magic remote 
        control
- Check if content playback in accordance with playback time
- Check the synchronize of Sound and page

※ UI Button: Should be able to access by using navigation key and screen cursor
※ If playback control button is installed on remote control as well, the function should work the same by using remote control
※ If app is MHP type,
- Playback control key of remote control may not work

summary: LIVE Key
description:
[NetCast4.5/webOS1.0]
Remote control has LIVE key

steps.action: Execute app → Press LIVE key of remote control
steps.result:
When pressing LIVE key of remote control, app moves properly to Live TV

※ NetCast: Move to Live TV after terminating app
※ webOS: OK if app moves properly to Live TV (irrelevant whether app is terminated or not)

summary: Home video broadcast audio
description: GIVEN

steps.action: WHEN
steps.result: Home team's video player game stream is matched with home team's broadcast audio

summary: Start from the beginning archive playback - Hide Spoilers ON
description:
GIVEN the user is entitled for archive game playback
AND Hide Spoilers is turned ON
AND team feeds are mediaState=MEDIA_ARCHIVE
AND the feed contains a resume bookmark after the top of the 1st inning

steps.action:
WHEN the user selects a previously watched archive game
AND the user selects a previously watched team feed
AND the user selects the "Start From The Beginning" button
steps.result:
THEN playback starts at the game broadcast milestone
AND the elapsed time in the scrbber starts at 00:00, and counts up as the video plays
AND all inning markers for the game are not displayed in the scrubber bar
AND inning markers are displayed to the left of the playback head as each half inning milestone is crossed
AND the top of the inning is identified with a triangle pointing up, left of the inning number
AND the bottom of the inning is identified with a triangle pointing down, left of the inning number

summary: Full Size
description: GIVEN

steps.action: Execute app → Play video content
steps.result:
There are no margins around video and video appears in full size

※ Margins can be left if original ratio of content is 4:3
※ OK if user can change to full screen using Q.Menu (Settings)

ex) Check if Text is inputted by using Voice Search UI Button, when use the LG Virtual Keyboard

summary: Correct Text

steps.action: Execute app → Check TEXT
steps.result:
- Text output is proper without typos or broken text
- If app includes UI button or text longer than screen area, text is not cropped, and rolling or ellipsis sign appears
- If list/description contents are longer than screen area, scroll appears properly
- UI Button and text allignment is proper

summary: Adult Authentication
description: Adult contents are included

steps.action: Execute app → Access adult content → Check adult authentication process
steps.result:
Adult verification process is included when accessing adult content.
* The adult verification process must be stated in the Comments section and the UX scenario document.
e.g. PIN code method, parental locks, credit card verification process, etc.

- Adult content is approved if an adult verification process is in place
- Pornography content is strictly prohibited

summary: registerDevice passes device information
description: GIVEN a user launches the app

steps.action: WHEN registerDevice is called
steps.result:
THEN the "graphql" type "xhr" Payload contains "operationName", "query", "variables"
AND "variables" contains "clientType", "device", "experience"
AND "device" contains "applicationRuntime", "attributes", "deviceFamily", "deviceLanguage", "deviceProfile", "knownDeviceId"
AND "attributes" contains "browserName", "browserVersion", "manufacturer", "model", "operatingSystem", "operatingSystemVersion"

summary: registerDevice is called for Free users
description: GIVEN a free user is already logged in

steps.action:
WHEN a free user launches the app
AND are in the MLB experience
steps.result:
THEN clients should make a registerDevice request to Media-Gateway
AND can be verified by searching "graphql" in the Network tab of browser dev tools
AND "clientExperience", "deviceId", "entitlements", and "sessionId" are present in the "registerDevice" call
AND an "accessToken" is present within "mlbAuth" in Redux

summary: 6 devices do trip concurrency
description:
Given a user has signed into 6 different devices and is actively streaming a game on all 5 devices.

This should include Live Games, Archived Games, VOD, SVOD, now RSN [https://baseball.atlassian.net/browse/COW-777|https://baseball.atlassian.net/browse/COW-777|smart-link] - and MLBN?? 

The messaging they should receive was updated here [https://baseball.atlassian.net/browse/COW-1216|https://baseball.atlassian.net/browse/COW-1216|smart-link]

steps.action: WHEN this user attempts to play a game on the 6th device
steps.result:
Then the user is presented with an error message stating the following: 

*Streaming Limit Reached*
There are too many devices using your [MLB.TV|http://MLB.TV] account right now. To log out all devices currently on your account, please go to [+MLB.com+|http://mlb.com/] and change your password.

And they are unable to stream a game on that 6th device until they stop streaming on one of the other devices.

summary: Same session is resumed after app is backgrounded
description:
**NOTE:** This must be tested on a kit through the Remote Inspector, as there is no way to invoke backgrounding through the browser. Can be confirmed via the Remote Inspector Console, "app/setIsBackgrounded", "next state", "experience", "MLB", "sessionId". Be sure to grab the sessionId when the app is open, before backgrounding.

GIVEN a user backgrounds their app for an hour

steps.action: WHEN the user returns
steps.result: THEN the previous session is resumed

summary: Hitting initPlaybackSession endpoint
description:
GIVEN the ps5-config contains featureFlags->experience->useMLB:true

**NOTE**
Config was updated in PROD

steps.action: WHEN live or archive game playback is initiated
steps.result:
THEN graphql network call request url is media-gateway.mlb.com/graphql
AND the Payload tab for the graphql call contains operationName:"initPlaybackSession"
AND "playback" is a field in the "initPlaybackSession" response
AND the following fields are present in the request, "cdn", "expiration", "token", and "url"

summary: Remote App Function - Samsung
description:
GIVEN I have a Samsung Account
GIVEN I have Wi-Fi enabled on the smartphone and on the SAME Wi-Fi network as your Samsung TV
GIVEN I have the SmartThings app installed on the smartphone

steps.action:
WHEN I Press the Red Power button 
AND I Open the SmartThings App on the smartphone
AND I tap the "Device" tab
AND I tap the "Home" icon
AND I tap to select a location where the Samsung TV is located
AND I tap the "Add" + sign
AND I tap "TV" under the "By device type" tab
AND I tap "Samsung"
AND I tap "TV" again
AND I tap "Start"
AND I tap to select a Location and Room
AND I tap "Next"
AND I tap to select the Samsung TV
AND I tap "OK" 
AND I tap "Done"
steps.result:

THEN I should notice that I can now use the Smartphone to control the Samsung TV

GIVEN TTS is turned ON
AND the user is logged in
AND a Free Game of the Day is on the schedule
AND the user is on the Games page

steps.action: WHEN focus is assigned to a FGotD game tile that has not gone live yet
steps.result: THEN the following statement is spoken, "Game <x> of <y>, <away_team>, <away_team_wins> Wins, <away_team_losses> Losses at <home_team>, <home_team_wins> Wins, <home_team_losses> Losses, <game_start_time> Game Start Time, Free Game of the Day, Pitching for the <away_team> <away_starting_pitcher>, <away_pitcher_wins> Wins, <away_pitcher_losses> Losses, <away_pitcher_ERA> Earned Run Average, Pitching for the <home_team> <home_starting_pitcher>, <home_pitcher_wins> Wins, <home_pitcher_losses> Losses, <home_pitcher_ERA> Earned Run Average. Press select to play game. Move <appropriate_directions> to hear more games. Move up to navigate to a new date."

summary: Game Tile Pregame Hide Spoilers ON
description:
GIVEN TTS is turned ON
AND the user is logged in
AND Hide Spoilers is turned ON
AND the user is on the Games page

steps.action: WHEN the user assigns focus to a game tile before the game is live
steps.result: THEN the following statement is spoken, "Game <x> of <y>, <away_team> at <home_team>, <game_start_time> Game Start Time, Press select to play game. Move <appropriate_directions> to hear more games. Move up to navigate to a new date."

summary: Top row Sunday
description:
GIVEN TTS is turned ON
AND the user is on the Games page
AND the user has selected the Calendar

steps.action: WHEN focus is assigned to Sun in the top row of the calendar
steps.result: THEN the following statement is spoken, "<day_of_the_week>, <month>, <day>, <year>, <number_of_games_on_this_day>. Press down or right to navigate to more dates, press up to navigate to a different month or to Today's games."

summary: Game Tile Live Not Entitled Hide Spoilers ON
description:
GIVEN TTS is turned ON
AND the user is not logged in
AND Hide Spoilers is turned ON
AND a Live game is on the schedule
AND the user is on the Games page

steps.action: WHEN focus is assigned to a Live game tile with a Lock icon
steps.result: THEN the following statement is spoken, "Game <x> of <y>, <away_team> at <home_team>, Not Entitled. Press select to play game. Move <appropriate_directions> to hear more games. Move up to navigate to a new date."

summary: Middle rows Saturday

steps.action: WHEN focus is assigned to Sat in a middle row of the calendar
steps.result: THEN the following statement is spoken, "<day_of_the_week>, <month>, <day>, <year>, <number_of_games_on_this_day>. Press up, down or left to navigate to more dates."

summary: TTS | Follow Team page load, top middle team set
description:
GIVEN TTS is enabled
AND the user has the Athletics set as the Following Team
AND the user is on the Settings page

steps.action: WHEN the user selects Following Teams
steps.result: THEN the app reader should say the following upon page load : 'The title reads: Add more teams to your clubhouse and help build your home screen on MLB.TV. You are following Athletics. Teams are organized in alphabetical order from left to right, and top to bottom. Press select to select a team to follow. You can follow more than one team. Once done, your selections will be saved and you can press or scroll left to navigate to the “Done” button. You can follow multiple teams. The {highlighted team} is highlighted. Press select to save this team as your Follow team. Move left, right, or down to hear additional teams to follow.'

summary: TTS | Navigation options top middle
description:
GIVEN TTS is enabled
AND the user is on the Favorite Team page

steps.action: WHEN the user scrolls to highlight the Athletics
steps.result: THEN the app should say 'The {selected team} is highlighted. Press select to save this team as your favorite team. Move left, right, or down to hear additional teams to favorite.'

AND the user is logged in

Help personalize your experience by selecting the team you go to bat for. Teams are organized in alphabetical order from left to right, and top to bottom. Press select to select a team to favorite. Press select to save this team as your favorite team. Move left, down, or up to hear another team to favorite. There is nothing selectable to the right.'

AND the app loads as expected

summary: TTS | Login Page Load with Email Address field highlighted
description:
GIVEN an unauthenticated user selects the Login button
AND the user has TTS Enabled

steps.action: WHEN the Login Page loads
steps.result:
THEN the Email Address field should be highlighted 
AND the app should communicate: “There are three ways to login on this page. You are currently highlighting the email field. You may also use your phone to login via QR code; users also have the option to visit mlb.com/activate and enter the 8 digit code on your screen. Your code is {code}."

**NOTE** : The express Login only applies if the app being tested has that feature.

summary: Following Teams - Settings page Cancel
description:
GIVEN a logged in user is on the Settings page
AND the user selects "Following Teams" in the list of settings
AND the user selects a following team
OR the user does not select a following team

steps.action: WHEN the user selects the "Cancel" button
steps.result:
THEN the user is navigated back to the Settings page
AND focus is assigned to "Log Out" in the list of settings
AND the user's previously saved following team/s are shown on the Following Teams page
AND the user's previously saved following team/s displays with secondary priority to the user's favorite team in the top row on the Games page, on day's the followed team is playing
AND the user's previously saved following team/s displays with secondary priority to the user's favorite team in the Games carousel, or Hero/Featured spot, on the Home page, on days the followed team is playing
IF a favorite team was not previously selected, the Games page, Games carousel, and Hero/Featured spot will first display any Following Teams the user may have selected
IF no favorite or following team is selected, games will display in order of game time

summary: Select favorite/follow  (Skip) Existing Account w/o favorite/follow
description:
GIVEN the user has an existing MLB Account
AND the existing MLB Account does not have saved favorite and following teams
AND the user is launching the app for the first time
AND the user selects the "Getting Started" button

steps.action:
WHEN the user selects a favorite team
AND the user selects the "Skip" button
AND the user selects at least 1 following team
AND the user selects the "Skip" button
AND the user logs in with their existing account
steps.result: THEN no favorite or following teams are saved to the existing account profile

summary: TTS | Focus on Uncover Password button
description:
GIVEN an unauthenticated user is on the Login Page
AND the user has TTS Enabled

steps.action: WHEN the user shifts focus to the Uncover Password button
steps.result: THEN the app reads "The password is currently hidden. Hit select to unhide your password. Scroll or hit left to edit your password. Scroll down to cancel your login.”

summary: Log In - Paid subscriber

steps.result:
THEN the subscriber is successfully logged in
AND the header contains the MLB logo and text left justified
AND "Home" displays to the right of the MLB logo and text
AND "Games" displays to the right of "Home"
AND "Settings" is displayed right justified

summary: TTS | Email Entry

OR the user selects to enter an Email
OR the user complete their Email entry
steps.result: THEN the app reader says "The email field is selected. Please hit select to enter the email address associated with your MLB.TV account. Press or scroll down to enter your password."

summary: TTS | Select Uncover Password button

steps.action: WHEN the user selects the Unhide Password button
steps.result: THEN the app reader says "The password is currently unhidden. Hit select to hide your password. Scroll or hit left to edit your password. Scroll down to cancel your login.”

summary: Following Teams - UI
description: GIVEN the MLB app has been launched

steps.action: WHEN the Following Teams page is displayed
steps.result:
THEN the left third of the screen contains text, buttons, and the MLB Logo
AND "Select additional teams to follow" is displayed as the title text (config driven)
AND "Add more teams to your clubhouse and help build your home screen on MLBTV." is the text that displays below the title text (config driven)
AND two buttons display below the text
AND the left button is labeled "Cancel" or "Skip"
AND the right button is labeled "Done" or "Next"
AND the MLB logo is top left aligned
AND the right two-thirds of the screen contains 3 columns of 10 scrollable tiles
AND the tile backgrounds are dark grey
AND all 30 teams are displayed in alphabetical order
AND each team logo and team name is displayed in its own tile
AND with focus on a tile in the top three rows, 3 rows of 3 tiles are displayed with a 4th row peaking from the bottom of the screen
AND as the user navigates down through the teams, all 3 columns scroll up
AND with focus on any tile in rows 4 through 8, 3 rows of 3 tiles are displayed with rows peaking from the top of the screen and bottom of the screen
AND once the user navigates to the 9th row, 3 rows of 3 tiles are displayed with a 4th row peaking from the top of the screen
AND once the user navigates to the 10th row, 2 rows of 3 tiles are displayed with a 3rd row peaking from the top of the screen

**NOTE:**
"Cancel" and "Done" will display in the buttons if the Following Teams page is loaded from the Settings page. 
"Skip" and "Next" will display in the buttons if the Following Teams page is loaded as part of the onboarding flow.

summary: Following Teams - Onboarding Skip

AND the user has not selected a team tile

steps.action: WHEN the user selects the "Skip" button
steps.result: THEN the user is taken to the Welcome page

summary: Favorite Team - Navigation/Focus
description: GIVEN the MLB app is launched

steps.action:
WHEN the user is on the Favorite Team page
AND the user presses the up/down/left/right directional buttons on the controller
OR the users moves the left analog stick up/down/left/right
steps.result:
THEN focus is indicated with a white border around the team tiles
OR focus is indicated with a white background with black text for buttons
AND focus should be assigned to the user's favorite team, on page load
OR if the user does not have a favorite team set, focus is assigned to the top left most team tile (Arizona Diamondbacks), on page load
AND focus moves in relation to the user's input on the controller
AND focus does not leave the first row if the user attempts to navigate up, beyond the first row
AND focus does not leave the right column if the user attempts to navigate right, beyond the right column
AND focus does not leave the last row if the user attempts to navigate down, beyond the last row
AND focus moves to the "Done"/"Next" button if the user navigates left from any tile in the left column
AND focus moves to the previously focused tile if the user navigates right from the "Done"/"Next" button
AND focus moves to the "Cancel"/"Skip" button if the user navigates left from the "Done"/"Next" button 
AND focus moves to the "Done"/"Next" button if the user navigates right from the "Cancel"/"Skip" button
AND focus does not move from the "Cancel"/"Skip" button if the user attempts to navigate further to the left
AND focus does not move from the "Cancel"/"Skip" button if the user attempts to navigate up or down
AND focus does not move from the "Done"/"Next" button if the user attempts to navigate up or down

**NOTE:** 
"Cancel" and "Done" will display in the buttons if the Favorite Team page is loaded from the Settings page. 
"Skip" and "Next" will display in the buttons if the Favorite Team page is loaded as part of the onboarding flow.
